entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Source of sleep log data being upserted into the database

entities.name: Sleep Session Tracker
entities.context: Python database layer that upserts sleep sessions and replaces sleep stages using SQLite

entities.name: sleep_sessions
entities.context: SQLite table storing sleep log metadata (log_id, date, start/end times, efficiency, source)

entities.name: sleep_stages
entities.context: SQLite table storing per-session sleep stage breakdowns (wake, light, deep, REM)

entities.name: Sleep Stage Classification
entities.context: Categorization of sleep into stages: wake, light, deep, REM — tracked with start time and duration

entities.name: Upsert Pattern
entities.context: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE pattern used to idempotently sync sleep data without duplicates

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine implied by SQL dialect (? placeholders, ON CONFLICT syntax)

relationships.context: Fitbit provides raw sleep log payloads that the tracker ingests and stores
Upserts sleep session records keyed by log_id
Replaces all stages for a given sleep_log_id on each upsert
Uses INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for idempotent data sync
Executes SQL via a connection cursor against a SQLite database
Each stage row maps to a classified sleep phase (wake/light/deep/rem) with timing data
One-to-many relationship: each session (log_id) has multiple stage records
